name: New Hebrides (1906-1980)

colors.symbolism: tradition and the indigenous people of the islands

description: A blue field with a central polychrome coat-of-arms badge of the New Hebrides Condominium.
history: The New Hebrides were jointly administered by the United Kingdom and France as a 'condominium' from the 1906 Anglo-French Convention until independence as Vanuatu on 30 July 1980. For most of this period the colony had no single official flag — the British and French national flags were flown side by side. A condominium badge bearing the joint coat of arms was adopted to mark the territory in colonial-era references and ensigns. The flag was retired on independence and replaced by the present flag of Vanuatu.

features.symbolism: the Pacific Ocean surrounding the New Hebrides archipelago

features.role: Coat of arms of the New Hebrides Condominium
features.symbolism: the joint badge of the Anglo-French Condominium of the New Hebrides, combining indigenous Melanesian motifs (notably the kava bowl and pig tusks) with administrative emblems of the joint British-French government
